ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Continued To Dec-10-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Brown for and with Angela Aviles. Chris Malloy appeared by telephone for Advance Inheritance. Priya Bahl (associate of Alex Borden) appeared by telephone.

Ms. Brown stated that the $30,000.00 portion of the extraordinary fees was separated out in the itemization, that there will be insufficient cash if fees are paid for Francisco's share, that the plot was not discovered until three months ago, that the sale price of the plot is less than one tenth of what the Decedent paid for it and that she does not know if it will be sold. Ms. Brown also stated that the third amendment includes all schedules for the assets on hand from May 1, 2012 to August 30, 2013, that there were three parcels of real property, that they sold the duplex, that the remaining two are condominiums, that the legal descriptions are included in the order, that she set forth collections of statutory fees in the November 7, 2013 document (page 39/exhibit 4), that they have all receipts for the $9,300.00 burial fee and that the one mutual fund at Wells Fargo Bank listed as cash in error was also corrected. Ms. Bahl stated that if the Court is inclined to grant the $30,000.00 fees, that they would like time to object. Mr. Malloy stated that Francisco is his Client and that he referred Francisco to Mr. Borden. Furthermore, Ms. Brown stated that there is a $13,000.00 short fall, that Francisco has gone missing, that her Client does not want to share title with non-family members, asked that the property not be distributed in kind, requested that the condos be given to Angie and the Brother, that cash be given to the other Beneficiaries, and stated that the Client and the Client's Accountant are responsible for the late taxes ($633.00 penalty and late fee). Mr. Malloy also stated that Francisco has fallen off their radar. The Court informed Counsel that they need 15 days notice of the third amendment to everyone. The Court also stated that the Creditor's Claims should be paid and that it will look at the possibility of payment of the taxes as well. The accounting petition was continued to December 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any further documents are to be filed by December 3, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)
